ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for Nanyang curry lion's mane mushroom bread bowl specifically. FOODON_03315116 (bread bowl food product) is the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (wheat bread, filled/topped) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for curry-filled bread bowls with mushroom in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one bread bowl (roughly 420 g total), comprising a hollowed sourdough or white bread bowl filled with approximately 250–280 g of Nanyang curry soup containing lion's mane mushroom.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: wheat bread (USDA FoodData Central ID 325871), coconut milk curry base (USDA FoodData Central ID 170172 and FAO/INFOODS SEA curry reference), lion's mane mushroom (USDA FoodData Central ID 169403)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Nanyang Curry Lion's Mane Mushroom Bread Bowl exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component model: approximately 45% bread bowl (wheat-based, ~190 g), 50% coconut-milk curry soup with lion's mane mushroom (~210 g), and 5% residual curry paste and aromatics (~20 g). Bread nutritional values from USDA SR Legacy (white/sourdough bread). Curry base estimated from USDA coconut milk entry and FAO/INFOODS SEA curry reference. Lion's mane mushroom values from USDA FoodData Central. Weighted average computed per 100 g of total dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and wheat bread products; analogous estimate for curry bread bowl composite
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nanyang Curry Lion's Mane Mushroom Bread Bowl. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a wheat-flour bread bowl (GI ~70–75 for white bread per University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the coconut-milk curry matrix (fat and protein content lower the effective GI of the composite meal). The composite GI of approximately 65 is consistent with published mixed-meal GI attenuation principles. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 77.7) / 100 ≈ 50.5, rounded to 51.0. Serving carbohydrate = 18.5 g/100 g × 420 g = 77.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~1302 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as one serving provides approximately 1302 mg sodium, representing over 56% of the WHO-recommended daily limit of 2000 mg, primarily from curry paste, coconut milk, and bread; portion reduction or a low-sodium curry adaptation is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~51 GL units per serving) driven by the wheat bread bowl
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this dish as the large wheat bread bowl contributes a high glycemic load of approximately 51 per serving; consuming only the curry filling without the bread bowl, or substituting a wholegrain bread bowl, would substantially reduce postprandial glucose impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from coconut milk (~4.5 g/100 g) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of saturated fat from coconut milk (approximately 18.9 g per serving) and high sodium (~1302 mg per serving) warrants moderation; choosing a reduced-coconut-milk curry base and limiting bread bowl consumption can help manage cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nanyang Curry Lion's Mane Mushroom Bread Bowl is a Singaporean-fusion dish that combines the bold, aromatic flavours of Nanyang (South Seas) curry with the meaty, seafood-like texture of lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus), all served inside a hollowed-out baked bread bowl. The term 'Nanyang' refers to the Southeast Asian culinary tradition brought by Chinese immigrants to Singapore and the Malay Archipelago, characterised by the use of coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, and chilli in a rich, fragrant curry base. Lion's mane mushroom is prized for its fibrous, tender texture that mimics seafood or chicken, making it a popular plant-based protein alternative in modern Singaporean cuisine. The bread bowl — typically made from sourdough or white wheat bread — absorbs the curry sauce, creating a satisfying combination of crisp exterior and sauce-soaked interior. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in protein (~4.2 g/100 g), relatively high in carbohydrates (~18.5 g/100 g) from the bread component, and contains significant saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g) from coconut milk. Sodium content is high (~310 mg/100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is high (~51), primarily driven by the wheat bread bowl, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Lion's mane mushroom contributes dietary fibre and bioactive beta-glucans. This dish is vegetarian and halal-friendly, and is served at specialty cafes and fusion restaurants in Singapore such as Flavours by Saute at Funan Mall.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Nanyang Curry Lion's Mane Mushroom Bread Bowl exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA tables. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component model using approved USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA sources for individual components (wheat bread, coconut milk curry base, lion's mane mushroom). GI is an analogous estimate based on the wheat bread bowl GI from the University of Sydney GI Database, moderated for the composite meal matrix. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated (not directly measured) from USDA FoodData Central and FAO/INFOODS SEA references; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB Singapore Foodo entry would upgrade confidence to high. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this dish contains no hydrogenated fats; coconut milk and bread do not contribute trans fats under standard preparation. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as all main ingredients (bread, coconut milk, lion's mane mushroom) are plant-derived and contain no dietary cholesterol. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients contributing B12. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 1.2 g from bread and coconut oil fractions). 6. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference (GI ~70–75) attenuated by fat and protein in the curry matrix; this is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement if available. 7. Serving size of 420 g is an estimate based on typical bread bowl dishes (bread bowl ~190 g + curry filling ~230 g); actual restaurant portion may vary. 8. The dish is listed under cuisine tag 'Global' in the source field survey record; reclassified here as 'Singaporean-Fusion' to reflect its Nanyang culinary heritage. 9. All -1 values in per_100g fields use the sentinel convention for nutrients not reported in the component sources used for estimation; these are not confirmed zeros but rather unreported values in the estimation model.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed with component-estimated nutrients, multilingual names for all mandatory Singaporean languages, GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database, and three health context conditions.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list — all entries confirmed present verbatim. Verified GI category threshold (GI 65 = medium, 56–69 range). Verified zero-value justifications for trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and vitamin_b12_mcg in notes. Verified all ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Confirmed no -1 sentinel values lack documentation in notes. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
